sources.notes: Find numbers whose factorial-related trailing-zero count meets the required relation.
Official editorial bounds the search around powers of 5 and uses Legendre-style valuation of factorials.

annotation.summary: Solve a factorial trailing-zero condition by finding all integers whose count of factors of 5 in n! matches the required value.
annotation.constraints: The target can be large, but the trailing-zero function changes only at multiples of powers of 5, allowing bounded candidate checks.
annotation.core_idea: Trailing zeroes in n! are v5(n!) = sum floor(n/5^i). This function is monotone and jumps at multiples of powers of 5. Instead of scanning all n, locate the approximate range using powers of 5 or binary search, then brute-check the small set of candidates around the jump boundaries.
annotation.complexity: O(log answer) candidate generation and checks.

solution_variants.name: factorial-valuation-candidates
solution_variants.summary: Use the monotone formula for v5(n!) and check only candidate values around jump points.

tags.evidence: The solution is based on p-adic divisibility counts of n!, specifically the exponent of 5.
A monotone trailing-zero function can be binary-searched to locate the small candidate interval.
After locating the interval, only a bounded number of candidates are explicitly tested.
